front: Composer: Armenian-American; wrote 67 symphonies; most famous is No. 2, 'Mysterious Mountain,' commissioned by and premiered under Leopold Stokowski of the Philadelphia Orchestra
Composer: John Cage witnessed the debut of his piece Loudaszak, which may have inspired Cage's aleatoric style
Symphony: No. 1 ('Exile') commemorates the Armenian Genocide; three movements titled 'Lament,' 'Conflict,' and 'Triumph'
Symphony: No. 21, dedicated to the Catholicos of Etchmiadzin (Vasken); features that city's bells of war across three movements
Symphony: No. 22 ('City of Light'), commissioned by the Birmingham Symphony; opens with five D major chords, but the vibraphone plays a dissonant F natural on the third chord
Symphony: No. 50 ('Mount St. Helens'); solo flute bridges the 'dawn theme' and the eruption in the final movement; second movement nicknamed 'Spirit Lake'
Tone Poem: incorporates actual whale sound recordings juxtaposed with major pentatonic melodies; title from the Book of Genesis; one of the earliest works to promote saving whales
back: And God Created Great Whales (Alan Hovhaness)
